summary: Book's biggest risk stays distribution overhang, not a single blow-up: 3 of 5 holdings carry live dilution/distribution capacity (UUUU undrawn $300M ATM + escalating Chalmers Form 144 selling, WRAP $200M baby-shelf, NTSK post-lockup VC distribution), and this 30-min re-check confirms NO fresh offering drew on any held name (UUUU ATM still just the 6/13 program, WRAP's only new item is the positive Frenel/NATO distribution deal); the one NEW item is a DVLT wildcard-sleeve broker/ledger desync (broker no longer reports the position) that the reconciler and a human should resolve before P&L silently diverges.

WRAP.note: $200M universal baby-shelf standing (last S-3 activity Feb 2026) on a sub-$100M post-pump name = drip-dilution CAPACITY only; re-check confirms NO July 424B5 draw (a 424B5 that surfaced under a WRAP web search is Ouster's, CIK 1816581). Only fresh news is the 7/8-7/10 Frenel/NATO WrapShield distribution deal (positive) + CEO open-market buying. FinBERT -0.853 'unusual volume' item is noise. Nightly audit source_mismatch/stale on WRAP is a NON-held cache row (held=false), immaterial to our stop. Position +$2.55 at $2.17.

UUUU.note: $300M ATM (2026-06-13) confirmed still undrawn this cycle -- no July prospectus draw -- but insider picture is net-negative by size: Chalmers Form 144 stack via RBC (200k 6/29, 100k/200k 7/2, +300k 7/9 ~ $10M+ at ~$13) outweighs CEO Bhappu's real 74k-sh/$968k buy (7/7) + Hansen (7/8). Lorentzian k-NN -0.75, FinBERT 0.0. Position -$1.36 at $13.23.

CAI.note: Fundamentals clean (director $990k buy 7/13, Caris Detect launch, $100M buyback, no shelf/ATM). Nightly audit (08:18) flagged broker_disagrees (broker $17.50 vs cache $16.99, 3%) -- watched intraday: broker converged to ~$17.00 vs cache/radar $16.99, so stops/P&L are NOT desynced. FinBERT +0.861. Position -$0.63 at $16.99.

NTSK.note: Book's best gainer +$6.72 (~$14.15) on an intact breakout / fresh 52-wk high, but post-IPO-lockup distribution persists: Form 144 1.75M Class A (7/8) + Lightspeed conversions (7/9); ICONIQ's 610k open-market buy (7/8) only partly offsets. Re-check found NO 424B5/secondary. Hiring pulse healthy (153 openings, no flag).

YEXT.note: No shelf/ATM/dilution filing; the failed Feb-2026 take-private was replaced by an anti-dilutive $140M self-tender buyback. Hiring pulse 24 openings, no flag. No fresh negative catalyst. Position +$0.43 at $5.43.

flags.why: Wildcard-sleeve position (18.28 sh) that today's broker reconciliation shows as LEDGER-ONLY -- broker no longer reports it, so the ledger may be desynced from the account of record; also on the desk avoid list as a serial diluter with active pump activity (Mandela Dollar stablecoin 7/10, Dream Bowl meme-coin listing 7/6). Human/reconciler should confirm whether it was actually closed BEFORE the numbers stop matching; do not re-buy.

Distressed reverse-merger pop: definitive Serapha Bio merger + $230M concurrent private placement leaves pre-merger holders only ~3.7% of the combined entity; oncology pipeline halted, ~75% workforce cut. Still appears on the scan.

Most recent 10-Q discloses going-concern doubt including possible Chapter 7 liquidation; just closed a $23.7M private placement (warrant overhang). FinBERT -0.288 on the cash-runway story. Keep hard-vetoed if it resurfaces.

Top non-held scan name (5.6, below line) with a textbook pump-and-dump signature: HK microcap ran ~$0.23 (6/12) to >$10 (6/16) then collapsed to ~$3.61, no fundamental driver. Reject on the pump veto; nightly audit also flags cache stale.

Non-held scan name (5.2, below line): 12-for-1 reverse split effective 7/14 (new CUSIP G8071C137) purely for Nasdaq $1 compliance, on an unfunded AI+blockchain+crypto/stablecoin pivot pump. Reverse-split-plus-crypto-pump, not a catalyst. FinBERT +0.919 is a misattributed generic-market headline.

Scan candidate (5.17) that diluted ~30%: $50M underwritten registered direct (27,624,310 sh at $1.81) + $9.2M Lilly private placement priced 6/25/closed 6/26, plus a 7/6 F-3 registering 3,030,303 PIPE sh for resale. Stock ~$1.72 sits BELOW the $1.81 offer; the CEO's 7/7 buy came AFTER the raise.

Scan candidate (below line) with a severe dilution overhang: ~167M sh out, active $300M S-3 shelf (7/8), 13.95M warrants, authorized shares hiked to 450M. Phase 2 news constructive but capital structure unresolved. On research avoid list.

On the daily watchlist but a pending ~$5.8B all-stock acquisition of Valaris (15.235 RIG sh per Valaris sh) dilutes existing holders to ~53% of the combined company and carries a DOJ 'second request' -- parties won't certify before 7/31, earliest close ~9/29. Material dilution + binary-vote risk; intel concurs do-not-chase but still carries it on the watchlist.

Watchlist name that priced a $100M underwritten offering (13,495,277 sh at $7.41) on 6/24 -- real dilution alongside the ABS-201 Phase 1 catalyst; ~$10.34 sits above mean analyst PT. FinBERT -0.941 on a -9% day. Keep flagged so it is not bought as a fresh breakout.
Automatic S-3ASR shelf effective 2026-01-13, already used twice via 424B5, plus an active $50M ATM -- ongoing drip-dilution setup. On the research avoid list.

Live $100M ATM (since 2024) plus a Feb-2026 $107.6M underwritten offering (20M sh at $5.00), now underwater near $3.73-3.83. Desk avoid list; not held. Nightly audit also flags a 2.7% source_mismatch (cache 3.825 vs yahoo 3.725).

Distressed: down ~91% YoY near 52-wk lows despite a token insider buy, 10-for-1 reverse split this cycle tied to Nasdaq compliance, pivoting into Dogecoin mining hardware -- distress signal, not a turnaround. On research avoid list.

dilution_watch.note: $300M ATM usable now; 30-min re-check shows NO July draw. Insider selling escalating: Chalmers Form 144s via RBC -- 200k (6/29), 100k/200k (7/2), +300k (7/9); outweighs CEO Bhappu's 74k-sh buy by size.

$200M universal baby-shelf for a sub-$100M post-pump name; re-check confirms NO July 424B5 draw (a 424B5 surfaced under a WRAP search is Ouster's, CIK 1816581). Standing capacity only; recent insider activity is BUYING.
$50M underwritten registered direct (27,624,310 sh at $1.81) + $9.2M Lilly private placement; closed 6/26. 7/6 F-3 registers 3,030,303 PIPE sh for resale. No NEW July offering.
~$5.8B all-stock Valaris acquisition; existing holders diluted to ~53% of combined co; DOJ second request pushes earliest close to ~9/29.
$100M underwritten offering, 13,495,277 sh at $7.41; immediate dilution.
Definitive Serapha Bio merger + $230M concurrent private placement; existing holders diluted to ~3.7% pro forma.
$300M shelf plus 13.95M warrants; authorized shares hiked to 450M -- severe overhang.
$23.7M private placement; going-concern disclosure in most recent 10-Q.
Automatic shelf effective, used twice via 424B5; $50M ATM active.
$107.6M underwritten offering now underwater; $100M ATM remains active.

disagreements: DVLT (wildcard sleeve): today's reconciliation shows the broker no longer reports the 18.28-sh position while the ledger still carries it -- a silent ledger/broker desync. Intel/daily-research flags it as ledger-only but keeps it as a live avoid-list pump (Mandela Dollar stablecoin, Dream Bowl meme-coin); I escalate it to a reconciliation flag so a human confirms the actual position state before P&L diverges.
Overtrading (carried, worse): radar shows weekly buy budget 9/4 used -- more than double the 4-buy weekly cap. Surface as a standalone discipline flag independent of any single ticker.

UUUU: intel frames the Bhappu/Hansen insider BUYS prominently and carries 'no boost'; verified this cycle the Chalmers Form 144 SALE stack (~800k sh listed 6/29-7/9 via RBC, ~$10M+) outweighs the ~$1M of buying by size, so the insider signal behind the score is net-NEGATIVE -- reinforces my elevated grade.
RIG: intel tags the Valaris deal 'do-not-chase' yet still carries RIG on the active watchlist; I keep it an elevated DO-NOT-BUY given ~53% dilution and a binary vote/antitrust event.

HDSN (watchlist buy candidate): checks out CLEAN, so NOT flagged -- effective but UNUSED $100M S-3 (12/16/2025) on a profitable name (Q1 rev $60.2M, +9%) with 10%-owner Hartree and insiders BUYING; DLA contract extended to 11/29/26. Note only that the buy score should reflect a standing (unused) shelf exists.

AVO (Mission Produce): intel carries it as a top non-held scan name on a +5.0 insider score; that signal is GlobalHarvest/Jay Pack takeover-style accumulation that triggered a poison-pill rights plan, NOT management conviction -- the insider component is mis-attributed. Below the buy line so no flag.
